title: Touchmicro.Inc Reports Record Q1 2026 Revenue Growth of 34%
excerpt: Touchmicro.Inc today announced record quarterly revenue of $2.8 billion, driven by strong demand for AI-powered grid systems and enterprise cloud solutions. The company's strategic pivot toward sustainable infrastructure has yielded significant margin improvements across all business segments.
content:
Touchmicro.Inc today announced record quarterly revenue of $2.8 billion for Q1 2026, representing a 34% year-over-year increase driven by strong demand for AI-powered grid systems and enterprise cloud solutions.

The company's strategic pivot toward sustainable infrastructure has yielded significant margin improvements across all business segments. Gross margins expanded to 42.3%, up from 38.1% in the prior year period.

"Our Q1 results demonstrate the power of our integrated platform strategy," said CEO Maria Chen. "The convergence of AI, cloud computing, and sustainable energy creates a massive addressable market, and Touchmicro is uniquely positioned to capture this opportunity."

Key Highlights:
- Revenue: $2.8B (+34% YoY)
- Gross Margin: 42.3% (up 420bps)
- Operating Income: $680M (+52% YoY)
- Free Cash Flow: $520M
- New Enterprise Contracts: 1,240 (+28% YoY)

The AI Grid Solutions segment was the standout performer, growing 67% year-over-year to $890M in revenue. Enterprise Cloud Services contributed $1.1B, while the Infrastructure segment generated $810M.

The company raised its full-year 2026 revenue guidance to $11.2-11.6 billion, up from the previous range of $10.5-11.0 billion. Adjusted EBITDA guidance was raised to $3.2-3.4 billion.

Touchmicro also announced a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, payable April 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 31, 2026.
author: Corporate Communications

title: Touchmicro.Inc Achieves Carbon Neutral Operations Across Global Facilities
excerpt: Touchmicro.Inc announced today that all global operations have achieved carbon neutrality, two years ahead of its 2028 target. The milestone was reached through a combination of renewable energy procurement, operational efficiency improvements, and verified carbon offset programs.
content:
Touchmicro.Inc announced today that all global operations have achieved carbon neutrality, two years ahead of its 2028 target. The milestone was reached through a combination of renewable energy procurement, operational efficiency improvements, and verified carbon offset programs.

The company's sustainability journey began in 2022 with the launch of its Net Zero 2028 initiative. Through aggressive investment in renewable energy and operational optimization, Touchmicro has reduced absolute emissions by 62% since the baseline year.

"Reaching carbon neutrality ahead of schedule validates our belief that sustainability and profitability are complementary goals," said Chief Sustainability Officer Dr. James Park. "Our green data centers consume 40% less energy than industry averages while delivering superior computing performance."

Key Sustainability Achievements:
- 100% renewable energy across all data centers
- 62% reduction in absolute emissions since 2022
- 40% improvement in data center energy efficiency (PUE: 1.08)
- Zero waste to landfill at 85% of facilities
- $450M invested in sustainability initiatives since 2022

Touchmicro's sustainability report, verified by an independent third party, will be published in full on March 30, 2026. The report follows GRI Standards and TCFD recommendations.

The company also announced the expansion of its Green Bond program, with $500M in new green bonds to fund renewable energy projects across Southeast Asia and Latin America.
author: Corporate Communications

title: Touchmicro.Inc Partners with Leading Semiconductor Firm on Next-Gen AI Chips
excerpt: Touchmicro.Inc and GlobalSilicon have entered into a strategic partnership to co-develop next-generation AI processors designed for edge computing and real-time data processing applications, with first commercial availability expected in Q4 2026.
content:
Touchmicro.Inc and GlobalSilicon have entered into a strategic partnership to co-develop next-generation AI processors designed for edge computing and real-time data processing applications, with first commercial availability expected in Q4 2026.

The partnership combines Touchmicro's AI software expertise and cloud infrastructure with GlobalSilicon's advanced semiconductor manufacturing capabilities. The jointly developed processors will feature a novel architecture optimized for inference workloads at the network edge.

"This partnership represents a paradigm shift in how AI is deployed at scale," said CTO Dr. Anika Patel. "By bringing computation closer to data sources, we can reduce latency by 10x while cutting energy consumption by 60% compared to centralized processing."

Key Partnership Details:
- Joint R&D investment of $1.2B over three years
- New chip architecture: TMX-7 Neural Processing Unit
- Target performance: 500 TOPS at 75W power envelope
- First commercial samples: Q4 2026
- Volume production: Q2 2027

The TMX-7 processor is designed specifically for Touchmicro's AI Grid platform, enabling real-time processing of sensor data, computer vision, and natural language understanding at the edge.

Early benchmark results show the TMX-7 delivering 3x the performance-per-watt of existing solutions, with a 65% smaller physical footprint. This makes it ideal for deployment in smart infrastructure, autonomous systems, and industrial IoT applications.

The partnership also includes a joint go-to-market strategy targeting enterprise customers in manufacturing, energy, healthcare, and transportation sectors.
author: Corporate Communications

title: Touchmicro.Inc Appoints Former Central Bank Governor to Board of Directors
excerpt: Touchmicro.Inc announced the appointment of Dr. Helena Vasquez, former Governor of the National Reserve Bank, to its Board of Directors. Dr. Vasquez brings over 30 years of experience in financial regulation, monetary policy, and institutional governance.
content:
Touchmicro.Inc announced the appointment of Dr. Helena Vasquez, former Governor of the National Reserve Bank, to its Board of Directors, effective February 1, 2026. Dr. Vasquez brings over 30 years of experience in financial regulation, monetary policy, and institutional governance.

Dr. Vasquez served as Central Bank Governor from 2018 to 2025, during which time she led the institution's digital transformation and oversaw the successful implementation of the country's Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) pilot program.

"Dr. Vasquez's deep expertise in financial systems, regulatory frameworks, and digital innovation makes her an exceptional addition to our Board," said Chairman Robert Liu. "Her perspective will be invaluable as Touchmicro continues to expand into financial technology and regulated industries."

Dr. Vasquez holds a Ph.D. in Economics from Stanford University and has served on the boards of the International Monetary Fund, the Bank for International Settlements, and the Financial Stability Board.

"I'm excited to join Touchmicro at this pivotal moment in the company's growth," said Dr. Vasquez. "The intersection of technology and finance presents extraordinary opportunities, and I look forward to contributing to the company's strategic direction."

With this appointment, Touchmicro's Board now consists of 11 members, with 7 independent directors. The Board's diversity metrics include 45% female representation and directors from 6 different countries.
author: Corporate Communications

title: Touchmicro.Inc Delivers Strong Q4 2025 Results, Full-Year Revenue Surpasses $9B
excerpt: Touchmicro.Inc reported Q4 2025 revenue of $2.5 billion, bringing full-year 2025 revenue to $9.2 billion. The company achieved record operating margins and announced a 15% increase in its quarterly dividend.
content:
Touchmicro.Inc reported Q4 2025 revenue of $2.5 billion, bringing full-year 2025 revenue to $9.2 billion, representing 28% year-over-year growth. The company achieved record operating margins of 24.8% and announced a 15% increase in its quarterly dividend.

Full-Year 2025 Highlights:
- Revenue: $9.2B (+28% YoY)
- Operating Margin: 24.8% (record)
- Net Income: $1.8B (+35% YoY)
- EPS: $4.12 (vs. $3.05 prior year)
- Free Cash Flow: $2.1B
- R&D Investment: $1.4B (15.2% of revenue)

Q4 2025 was highlighted by strong enterprise contract wins and the successful launch of the AI Grid 3.0 platform. Customer retention rates remained above 97%, with net revenue retention at 128%.

"2025 was a transformational year for Touchmicro," said CEO Maria Chen. "We delivered record financial results while making significant investments in AI, sustainability, and talent that position us for sustained long-term growth."

The Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, a 15% increase from the previous $0.156 per share. The dividend is payable February 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of January 31, 2026.

For 2026, the company provided initial guidance of $10.5-11.0 billion in revenue and adjusted EBITDA of $2.9-3.1 billion.
author: Corporate Communications

title: Touchmicro.Inc Announces $2B Cloud Infrastructure Expansion in Asia-Pacific
excerpt: Touchmicro.Inc announced plans to invest $2 billion in new cloud infrastructure across the Asia-Pacific region, including new data centers in Singapore, Tokyo, and Sydney, to meet surging enterprise demand for AI-powered cloud services.
content:
Touchmicro.Inc announced plans to invest $2 billion in new cloud infrastructure across the Asia-Pacific region over the next three years, including new data centers in Singapore, Tokyo, and Sydney, to meet surging enterprise demand for AI-powered cloud services.

The expansion will add over 500MW of computing capacity and create approximately 3,000 new jobs across the region. All new facilities will be powered by 100% renewable energy and built to the highest environmental standards.

"Asia-Pacific is the fastest-growing market for enterprise cloud and AI services," said EVP of Global Operations Sarah Kim. "This investment ensures we can serve our customers with low-latency, high-performance infrastructure right where they need it."

Expansion Details:
- Singapore: 200MW flagship campus (operational Q3 2027)
- Tokyo: 150MW enterprise hub (operational Q1 2027)
- Sydney: 150MW sustainability-first facility (operational Q4 2026)
- Total investment: $2B over 3 years
- New jobs: ~3,000 across all locations

The Singapore campus will be the company's largest facility in Asia and will serve as the regional hub for AI Grid processing. The Tokyo facility will focus on financial services and manufacturing clients, while the Sydney center will specialize in government and healthcare workloads.

All three facilities will achieve PUE (Power Usage Effectiveness) ratings below 1.10, setting new benchmarks for energy efficiency in the region.
author: Corporate Communications

title: Touchmicro.Inc Launches Global Innovation Lab Network for Quantum Computing Research
excerpt: Touchmicro.Inc unveiled its Global Innovation Lab Network, a $500 million initiative establishing quantum computing research centers in collaboration with leading universities across five continents.
content:
Touchmicro.Inc unveiled its Global Innovation Lab Network, a $500 million initiative establishing quantum computing research centers in collaboration with leading universities across five continents.

The network will comprise six research labs located at MIT, Oxford University, ETH Zurich, National University of Singapore, University of Tokyo, and University of São Paulo. Each lab will focus on specific aspects of quantum computing applicable to real-world enterprise challenges.

"Quantum computing will redefine what's possible in optimization, cryptography, drug discovery, and materials science," said CTO Dr. Anika Patel. "By partnering with the world's leading research institutions, we're accelerating the timeline to practical quantum advantage."

Research Focus Areas:
- Quantum error correction and fault-tolerant computing
- Hybrid quantum-classical algorithms for optimization
- Quantum machine learning and AI
- Post-quantum cryptography and security
- Quantum simulation for materials and drug discovery

The initiative includes 200 research positions, 50 postdoctoral fellowships, and a graduate scholarship program supporting 100 students annually. Total investment over five years is projected at $500 million.

Early results from pilot research have demonstrated quantum advantage in portfolio optimization problems, achieving solutions 1000x faster than classical methods for large-scale financial modeling scenarios.
author: Corporate Communications

title: Touchmicro.Inc Publishes 2025 Diversity & Inclusion Report Showing Industry-Leading Metrics
excerpt: Touchmicro.Inc released its annual Diversity & Inclusion report, revealing that the company has achieved 42% female representation in technical roles and 38% in senior leadership positions, surpassing industry averages.
content:
Touchmicro.Inc released its annual Diversity & Inclusion report, revealing that the company has achieved 42% female representation in technical roles and 38% in senior leadership positions, surpassing technology industry averages by a significant margin.

The report highlights significant progress across all diversity dimensions, including gender, ethnicity, disability, and LGBTQ+ inclusion. The company's workforce now represents 68 nationalities across 24 countries.

Key D&I Metrics for 2025:
- Female representation (overall): 44%
- Female representation (technical roles): 42%
- Female representation (senior leadership): 38%
- Ethnic diversity (leadership): 52% non-majority representation
- Pay equity ratio: 99.2% (gender), 98.8% (ethnicity)
- Employee satisfaction score: 4.6/5.0

"Diversity isn't just a value—it's a competitive advantage," said Chief People Officer Dr. Amara Williams. "Diverse teams build better products, make better decisions, and create more innovative solutions. Our metrics reflect years of intentional investment in inclusive hiring, development, and culture."

The company's D&I initiatives include:
- Touchmicro Academy: 5,000+ participants in technical upskilling programs
- Returnship Program: 200+ professionals returning from career breaks
- ERG Network: 12 Employee Resource Groups with 8,000+ members
- Community STEM: $25M invested in STEM education programs in underserved communities

Touchmicro was recognized by Forbes as one of the "World's Best Employers for Diversity" for the third consecutive year.
author: Corporate Communications
